summary: The Docker-based reproduction clones xmldom 0.9.9, mounts it into a Node 20 container, and confirms that parsing a deeply nested XML document crashes in Element.normalize() with RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause DOMParser.parseFromString() to terminate with a RangeError rooted in recursive Element.normalize() calls, demonstrating attacker-triggerable parser denial of service.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned https://github.com/xmldom/xmldom.git at commit 7ffb16bce1170c7314151379531147637ab772a1, built the reproduction image, and observed RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded from /target-repo/lib/dom.js during Element.normalize() while parsing a 20000-level nested XML payload. The verifier then reported "verification passed" and the run script exited successfully.
verification_method: The verifier executes node /app/exploit.js inside the container, expects the process to exit non-zero, and checks the captured output for both the RangeError message and an Element.normalize stack frame under /target-repo/lib/dom.js. This distinguishes the vulnerability from unrelated startup failures or generic container errors.
evidence_produced_by_run: The container printed "[evohunt] attempting parse with 20000 nested elements" before the failure.
The observed stderr contained "RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded".
The stack trace included repeated "at Element.normalize (/target-repo/lib/dom.js:...)" frames.
verify.sh printed "verification passed: deeply nested XML triggers recursive normalize() stack exhaustion".

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
